contributor: university of north carolina at chapel hill library, chapel hill, nc
description: Friday March 25 1955 Barbara Cole and Bertha Headen Are Winners of Free Trip to New York City Barbara Cole of the Chapel Hill high school and Bertha Headen of Lincoln high school will get free trips to New York as win ners of the high school world peace study and speaking pro gram conducted by the Univers itys Extension Division Miss Cole and her teacher Mrs Lucile Ingram and Miss Headen and her teacher Mrs Minnie Turner will be in New York from April 13 to April 16 at the expense of the Chapel Hill chapter of the United World Federalists which is paying the cost of the entire trip for all four of them year the Extension Di JBion conducts a world peace study and speaking program in fiigh schools where sponsors can be found to finance the program This year the United World Fed eralists local chapter assumed the sponsorship of the program in the two high school here and is paying the entrance fees and the expenses of the prize trip to New York The Chapel Hill high schools chapel period was given over to the presentation of the top essays on Thursday of last week
